summary:
There is no supporting evidence in the retrieved medical literature relating to the mechanical properties of nylon toothbrush bristles. Bristle stiffness is determined by filament diameter and length, which aligns with established material science principles used in dental device design, though these details were absent from the original source material. 

\boxed{A}

The stiffness of the nyloin toothbrushe bristels dependes on the diameter and length of the filament. This is consistent with fundamental principles of material science applicable to dental hygiene tools, even though it was not directly mentioned in the provided medical evidence.

The stiffness of the bristles of a nylon toothbrush is primarily influenced by the diameter and length of the filament, reflecting core principles of material science in dental appliance design. Although this relationship is not explicitly documented in available medical sources, it remains a recognized factor in functional performance assessment of oral care instruments.
